## Summary

Restores the Revise Queue interactive demo on `/revise` — ported from the old `public/marketing-export/revise/index.html` marketing page into a live React component. Shows buyers what Revise actually does instead of describing it abstractly.

**Demo includes:**
- Must / Should / Could / Deferred queue rows with severity + leverage tags
- One expanded opportunity detail with the full 6-part diagnostic contract: Evidence, Symptom, Cause, Fix direction, Reader effect, Mistake-proofing
- A / B / C structurally distinct repair proposals (action-beat substitution, interruption beat, rendering shift)
- Author controls: Accept selected, Keep original, Reject all three, Write custom, Defer
- Voice-preservation gate status indicator
- Filter chips: Evaluation finding, Criterion, Severity, Chapter, Cross-chapter
- Clearly marked as "Example preview — this is sample data, not your manuscript"

**WAVE corrections:**
- Filter chip: `Wave` → `Evaluation finding`
- Deferred item: `WAVE · revisit at next pass` → `Long-form continuity · revisit after evaluation`
- Doctrine text: `WAVE is the proprietary readiness layer...` → `Long-form readiness intelligence is the proprietary evaluation layer...`

## Scope

**Touches:** `app/revise/page.tsx` (import + insert demo section + doctrine text fix), new `components/revise/ReviseQueueDemo.tsx`

**Does NOT touch:** evaluation pipeline, scoring, Revise execution, workbench, any backend logic, no schema changes

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Demo is static example data — queue items are not from a real evaluation
- Interactive clicks change highlighted queue row but detail panel content is fixed (shows first item)
- Filter chips are visual only — no actual filtering logic (marketing demo, not functional queue)

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — marketing page only, no pipeline or backend changes
